Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Sorry to punt this back, but James N. is just trying to clean these up.

Patch Set 2:

> NB: ALWAYS_EXCEPT is for tests that are failing due to some defect.
 > Tests that are to be permanently skipped (eg. because of version interop)
 > should instead be skipped from within the test itself so that this section
 > only contains tests that need to be fixed.

James, if you get a chance, could you please confirm that this is true in the test scripts. We want to have ALWAYS_EXCEPT for tests that are temporarily disabled due to a regression, and tests that are permanently skipped due to configuration issues skipped in the test itself.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 2:

No prob, better to get it right.

Patch Set 3:

> James, if you get a chance, could you please confirm that this is true in the test scripts. We want to have ALWAYS_EXCEPT for tests that are temporarily disabled due to a regression, and tests that are permanently skipped due to configuration issues skipped in the test itself.

Andreas - I agree with this comment and it is true of all test suite's use of the ALWAYS_EXCEPT list with the possible exception of sanity-lfsck which adds tests to the ALWAYS_EXCEPT list based on server version.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I hate to throw this back again, but, when we skip a test for a client/server version, we use skip.

These should all be skip() instead of skip_env(). Andreas defined this recently for me as, we use skip_env to be: to skip a test during developer testing because some tool is missing, but fail the test in release testing because the test environment is not configured properly.

parents.subject: LU-11982 utils: Correct lfs migrate help

commit.subject: LU-10143 tests: Add version check for interop
commit.message:
LU-10143 tests: Add version check for interop

Without the fix for LU-10143, these tests will fail, so add
a version check for interop.

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I65eead5d5252ec6abf5d8de68d73e4f9b690d030
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/34285
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
